verses.latin_vulgate: Cantabo dilecto meo  canticum patruelis mei vineæ suæ.  Vinea facta est dilecto meo  in cornu filio olei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָשִׁ֤ירָה נָּא֙ לִֽידִידִ֔י שִׁירַ֥ת דּוֹדִ֖י לְכַרְמ֑וֹ כֶּ֛רֶם הָיָ֥ה לִידִידִ֖י בְּקֶ֥רֶן בֶּן־שָֽׁמֶן׃
verses.king_james: Now will I sing to my wellbeloved a song of my beloved touching his vineyard. My wellbeloved hath a vineyard in a very fruitful hill:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et sepivit eam, et lapides elegit ex illa,  et plantavit eam electam ;  et ædificavit turrim in medio ejus,  et torcular exstruxit in ea ;  et exspectavit ut faceret uvas,  et fecit labruscas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְעַזְּקֵ֣הוּ וַֽיְסַקְּלֵ֗הוּ וַיִּטָּעֵ֙הוּ֙ שֹׂרֵ֔ק וַיִּ֤בֶן מִגְדָּל֙ בְּתוֹכ֔וֹ וְגַם־יֶ֖קֶב חָצֵ֣ב בּ֑וֹ וַיְקַ֛ו לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת עֲנָבִ֖ים וַיַּ֥עַשׂ בְּאֻשִֽׁים׃
verses.king_james: And he fenced it, and gathered out the stones thereof, and planted it with the choicest vine, and built a tower in the midst of it, and also made a winepress therein: and he looked that it should bring forth grapes, and it brought forth wild grapes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc ergo, habitatores Jerusalem  et viri Juda,  judicate inter me  et vineam meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֛ה יוֹשֵׁ֥ב יְרוּשָׁלַ֖͏ִם וְאִ֣ישׁ יְהוּדָ֑ה שִׁפְטוּ־נָ֕א בֵּינִ֖י וּבֵ֥ין כַּרְמִֽי׃
verses.king_james: And now, O inhabitants of Jerusalem, and men of Judah, judge, I pray you, betwixt me and my vineyard.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid est quod debui ultra facere vineæ meæ,  et non feci ei ?  an quod exspectavi ut faceret uvas,  et fecit labruscas ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־לַּעֲשׂ֥וֹת עוֹד֙ לְכַרְמִ֔י וְלֹ֥א עָשִׂ֖יתִי בּ֑וֹ מַדּ֧וּעַ קִוֵּ֛יתִי לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת עֲנָבִ֖ים וַיַּ֥עַשׂ בְּאֻשִֽׁים׃
verses.king_james: What could have been done more to my vineyard, that I have not done in it? wherefore, when I looked that it should bring forth grapes, brought it forth wild grapes?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc ostendam vobis  quid ego faciam vineæ meæ :  auferam sepem ejus,  et erit in direptionem ;  diruam maceriam ejus,  et erit in conculcationem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּה֙ אוֹדִיעָה־נָּ֣א אֶתְכֶ֔ם אֵ֛ת אֲשֶׁר־אֲנִ֥י עֹשֶׂ֖ה לְכַרְמִ֑י הָסֵ֤ר מְשׂוּכָּתוֹ֙ וְהָיָ֣ה לְבָעֵ֔ר פָּרֹ֥ץ גְּדֵר֖וֹ וְהָיָ֥ה לְמִרְמָֽס׃
verses.king_james: And now go to; I will tell you what I will do to my vineyard: I will take away the hedge thereof, and it shall be eaten up; and break down the wall thereof, and it shall be trodden down:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ponam eam desertam ;  non putabitur et non fodietur :  et ascendent vepres et spinæ,  et nubibus mandabo  ne pluant super eam imbrem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲשִׁיתֵ֣הוּ בָתָ֗ה לֹ֤א יִזָּמֵר֙ וְלֹ֣א יֵעָדֵ֔ר וְעָלָ֥ה שָׁמִ֖יר וָשָׁ֑יִת וְעַ֤ל הֶֽעָבִים֙ אֲצַוֶּ֔ה מֵהַמְטִ֥יר עָלָ֖יו מָטָֽר׃
verses.king_james: And I will lay it waste: it shall not be pruned, nor digged; but there shall come up briers and thorns: I will also command the clouds that they rain no rain upon it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vinea enim Domini exercituum  domus Israël est ;  et vir Juda  germen ejus delectabile :  et exspectavi ut faceret judicium,  et ecce iniquitas ;  et justitiam, et ecce clamor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י כֶ֜רֶם יְהֹוָ֤ה צְבָאוֹת֙ בֵּ֣ית יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וְאִ֣ישׁ יְהוּדָ֔ה נְטַ֖ע שַׁעֲשׁוּעָ֑יו וַיְקַ֤ו לְמִשְׁפָּט֙ וְהִנֵּ֣ה מִשְׂפָּ֔ח לִצְדָקָ֖ה וְהִנֵּ֥ה צְעָקָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: For the vineyard of the Lord of hosts is the house of Israel, and the men of Judah his pleasant plant: and he looked for judgment, but behold oppression; for righteousness, but behold a cry.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ qui conjungitis domum ad domum,  et agrum agro copulatis  usque ad terminum loci !  Numquid habitabitis vos soli  in medio terræ ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֗וֹי מַגִּיעֵ֥י בַ֙יִת֙ בְּבַ֔יִת שָׂדֶ֥ה בְשָׂדֶ֖ה יַקְרִ֑יבוּ עַ֚ד אֶ֣פֶס מָק֔וֹם וְהוּשַׁבְתֶּ֥ם לְבַדְּכֶ֖ם בְּקֶ֥רֶב הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: Woe unto them that join house to house, that lay field to field, till there be no place, that they may be placed alone in the midst of the earth!

verses.latin_vulgate: In auribus meis sunt hæc,  dicit Dominus exercituum ;  nisi domus multæ desertæ fuerint,  grandes et pulchræ, absque habitatore.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּאׇזְנָ֖י יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֑וֹת אִם־לֹ֞א בָּתִּ֤ים רַבִּים֙ לְשַׁמָּ֣ה יִֽהְי֔וּ גְּדֹלִ֥ים וְטוֹבִ֖ים מֵאֵ֥ין יוֹשֵֽׁב׃
verses.king_james: In mine ears said the Lord of hosts, Of a truth many houses shall be desolate, even great and fair, without inhabitant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Decem enim jugera vinearum facient lagunculam unam,  et triginta modii sementis facient modios tres.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֗י עֲשֶׂ֙רֶת֙ צִמְדֵּי־כֶ֔רֶם יַעֲשׂ֖וּ בַּ֣ת אֶחָ֑ת וְזֶ֥רַע חֹ֖מֶר יַעֲשֶׂ֥ה אֵיפָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Yea, ten acres of vineyard shall yield one bath, and the seed of an homer shall yield an ephah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ qui consurgitis mane  ad ebrietatem sectandam,  et potandum usque ad vesperam,  ut vino æstuetis !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֛וֹי מַשְׁכִּימֵ֥י בַבֹּ֖קֶר שֵׁכָ֣ר יִרְדֹּ֑פוּ מְאַחֲרֵ֣י בַנֶּ֔שֶׁף יַ֖יִן יַדְלִיקֵֽם׃
verses.king_james: Woe unto them that rise up early in the morning, that they may follow strong drink; that continue until night, till wine inflame them!

verses.latin_vulgate: Cithara, et lyra, et tympanum,  et tibia, et vinum in conviviis vestris ;  et opus Domini non respicitis,  nec opera manuum ejus consideratis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֨ה כִנּ֜וֹר וָנֶ֗בֶל תֹּ֧ף וְחָלִ֛יל וָיַ֖יִן מִשְׁתֵּיהֶ֑ם וְאֵ֨ת פֹּ֤עַל יְהֹוָה֙ לֹ֣א יַבִּ֔יטוּ וּמַעֲשֵׂ֥ה יָדָ֖יו לֹ֥א רָאֽוּ׃
verses.king_james: And the harp, and the viol, the tabret, and pipe, and wine, are in their feasts: but they regard not the work of the Lord, neither consider the operation of his hands.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea captivus ductus est populus meus,  quia non habuit scientiam,  et nobiles ejus interierunt fame,  et multitudo ejus siti exaruit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֛ן גָּלָ֥ה עַמִּ֖י מִבְּלִי־דָ֑עַת וּכְבוֹדוֹ֙ מְתֵ֣י רָעָ֔ב וַהֲמוֹנ֖וֹ צִחֵ֥ה צָמָֽא׃
verses.king_james: Therefore my people are gone into captivity, because they have no knowledge: and their honourable men are famished, and their multitude dried up with thirst.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea dilatavit infernus animam suam,  et aperuit os suum absque ullo termino ;  et descendent fortes ejus,  et populus ejus, et sublimes gloriosique ejus, ad eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֗ן הִרְחִ֤יבָה שְּׁאוֹל֙ נַפְשָׁ֔הּ וּפָעֲרָ֥ה פִ֖יהָ לִבְלִי־חֹ֑ק וְיָרַ֨ד הֲדָרָ֧הּ וַהֲמוֹנָ֛הּ וּשְׁאוֹנָ֖הּ וְעָלֵ֥ז בָּֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Therefore hell hath enlarged herself, and opened her mouth without measure: and their glory, and their multitude, and their pomp, and he that rejoiceth, shall descend into it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et incurvabitur homo, et humiliabitur vir,  et oculi sublimium deprimentur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשַּׁ֥ח אָדָ֖ם וַיִּשְׁפַּל־אִ֑ישׁ וְעֵינֵ֥י גְבֹהִ֖ים תִּשְׁפַּֽלְנָה׃
verses.king_james: And the mean man shall be brought down, and the mighty man shall be humbled, and the eyes of the lofty shall be humbled:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exaltabitur Dominus exercituum in judicio ;  et Deus sanctus sanctificabitur in justitia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּגְבַּ֛הּ יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָא֖וֹת בַּמִּשְׁפָּ֑ט וְהָאֵל֙ הַקָּד֔וֹשׁ נִקְדָּ֖שׁ בִּצְדָקָֽה׃
verses.king_james: But the Lord of hosts shall be exalted in judgment, and God that is holy shall be sanctified in righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et pascentur agni juxta ordinem suum,  et deserta in ubertatem versa advenæ comedent.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְרָע֥וּ כְבָשִׂ֖ים כְּדׇבְרָ֑ם וְחׇרְב֥וֹת מֵחִ֖ים גָּרִ֥ים יֹאכֵֽלוּ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Then shall the lambs feed after their manner, and the waste places of the fat ones shall strangers eat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ qui trahitis iniquitatem  in funiculis vanitatis,  et quasi vinculum plaustri peccatum !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֛וֹי מֹשְׁכֵ֥י הֶעָוֺ֖ן בְּחַבְלֵ֣י הַשָּׁ֑וְא וְכַעֲב֥וֹת הָעֲגָלָ֖ה חַטָּאָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Woe unto them that draw iniquity with cords of vanity, and sin as it were with a cart rope:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui dicitis : Festinet,  et cito veniat opus ejus, ut videamus ;  et appropiet, et veniat  consilium sancti Israël,  et sciemus illud !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָאֹמְרִ֗ים יְמַהֵ֧ר ׀ יָחִ֛ישָׁה מַעֲשֵׂ֖הוּ לְמַ֣עַן נִרְאֶ֑ה וְתִקְרַ֣ב וְתָב֗וֹאָה עֲצַ֛ת קְד֥וֹשׁ יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל וְנֵדָֽעָה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: That say, Let him make speed, and hasten his work, that we may see it: and let the counsel of the Holy One of Israel draw nigh and come, that we may know it!

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ qui dicitis malum bonum,  et bonum malum ;  ponentes tenebras lucem,  et lucem tenebras ;  ponentes amarum in dulce,  et dulce in amarum !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֣וֹי הָאֹמְרִ֥ים לָרַ֛ע ט֖וֹב וְלַטּ֣וֹב רָ֑ע שָׂמִ֨ים חֹ֤שֶׁךְ לְאוֹר֙ וְא֣וֹר לְחֹ֔שֶׁךְ שָׂמִ֥ים מַ֛ר לְמָת֖וֹק וּמָת֥וֹק לְמָֽר׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Woe unto them that call evil good, and good evil; that put darkness for light, and light for darkness; that put bitter for sweet, and sweet for bitter!

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ qui sapientes estis in oculis vestris,  et coram vobismetipsis prudentes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֖וֹי חֲכָמִ֣ים בְּעֵינֵיהֶ֑ם וְנֶ֥גֶד פְּנֵיהֶ֖ם נְבֹנִֽים׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Woe unto them that are wise in their own eyes, and prudent in their own sight!

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ qui potentes estis ad bibendum vinum,  et viri fortes ad miscendam ebrietatem !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֕וֹי גִּבּוֹרִ֖ים לִשְׁתּ֣וֹת יָ֑יִן וְאַנְשֵׁי־חַ֖יִל לִמְסֹ֥ךְ שֵׁכָֽר׃
verses.king_james: Woe unto them that are mighty to drink wine, and men of strength to mingle strong drink:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui justificatis impium pro muneribus,  et justitiam justi aufertis ab eo !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַצְדִּיקֵ֥י רָשָׁ֖ע עֵ֣קֶב שֹׁ֑חַד וְצִדְקַ֥ת צַדִּיקִ֖ים יָסִ֥ירוּ מִמֶּֽנּוּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Which justify the wicked for reward, and take away the righteousness of the righteous from him!

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter hoc, sicut devorat stipulam lingua ignis,  et calor flammæ exurit,  sic radix eorum quasi favilla erit,  et germen eorum ut pulvis ascendet ;  abjecerunt enim legem Domini exercituum,  et eloquium sancti Israël blasphemaverunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵן֩ כֶּאֱכֹ֨ל קַ֜שׁ לְשׁ֣וֹן אֵ֗שׁ וַחֲשַׁ֤שׁ לֶהָבָה֙ יִרְפֶּ֔ה שׇׁרְשָׁם֙ כַּמָּ֣ק יִֽהְיֶ֔ה וּפִרְחָ֖ם כָּאָבָ֣ק יַעֲלֶ֑ה כִּ֣י מָאֲס֗וּ אֵ֚ת תּוֹרַת֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת וְאֵ֛ת אִמְרַ֥ת קְדֽוֹשׁ־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל נִאֵֽצוּ׃
verses.king_james: Therefore as the fire devoureth the stubble, and the flame consumeth the chaff, so their root shall be as rottenness, and their blossom shall go up as dust: because they have cast away the law of the Lord of hosts, and despised the word of the Holy One of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo iratus est furor Domini in populum suum,  et extendit manum suam super eum, et percussit eum :  et conturbati sunt montes,  et facta sunt morticina eorum quasi stercus in medio platearum.  In his omnibus non est adversus furor ejus,  sed adhuc manus ejus extenta.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־כֵּ֡ן חָרָה֩ אַף־יְהֹוָ֨ה בְּעַמּ֜וֹ וַיֵּ֣ט יָד֧וֹ עָלָ֣יו וַיַּכֵּ֗הוּ וַֽיִּרְגְּזוּ֙ הֶהָרִ֔ים וַתְּהִ֧י נִבְלָתָ֛ם כַּסּוּחָ֖ה בְּקֶ֣רֶב חוּצ֑וֹת בְּכׇל־זֹאת֙ לֹא־שָׁ֣ב אַפּ֔וֹ וְע֖וֹד יָד֥וֹ נְטוּיָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Therefore is the anger of the Lord kindled against his people, and he hath stretched forth his hand against them, and hath smitten them: and the hills did tremble, and their carcases were torn in the midst of the streets. For all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et elevabit signum in nationibus procul,  et sibilabit ad eum de finibus terræ :  et ecce festinus velociter veniet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנָֽשָׂא־נֵ֤ס לַגּוֹיִם֙ מֵרָח֔וֹק וְשָׁ֥רַק ל֖וֹ מִקְצֵ֣ה הָאָ֑רֶץ וְהִנֵּ֥ה מְהֵרָ֖ה קַ֥ל יָבֽוֹא׃
verses.king_james: And he will lift up an ensign to the nations from far, and will hiss unto them from the end of the earth: and, behold, they shall come with speed swiftly:

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est deficiens neque laborans in eo ;  non dormitabit, neque dormiet ;  neque solvetur cingulum renum ejus,  nec rumpetur corrigia calceamenti ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵין־עָיֵ֤ף וְאֵין־כּוֹשֵׁל֙ בּ֔וֹ לֹ֥א יָנ֖וּם וְלֹ֣א יִישָׁ֑ן וְלֹ֤א נִפְתַּח֙ אֵז֣וֹר חֲלָצָ֔יו וְלֹ֥א נִתַּ֖ק שְׂר֥וֹךְ נְעָלָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: None shall be weary nor stumble among them; none shall slumber nor sleep; neither shall the girdle of their loins be loosed, nor the latchet of their shoes be broken:

verses.latin_vulgate: Sagittæ ejus acutæ, et omnes arcus ejus extenti.  Ungulæ equorum ejus ut silex,  et rotæ ejus quasi impetus tempestatis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֤ר חִצָּיו֙ שְׁנוּנִ֔ים וְכׇל־קַשְּׁתֹתָ֖יו דְּרֻכ֑וֹת פַּרְס֤וֹת סוּסָיו֙ כַּצַּ֣ר נֶחְשָׁ֔בוּ וְגַלְגִּלָּ֖יו כַּסּוּפָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Whose arrows are sharp, and all their bows bent, their horses’ hoofs shall be counted like flint, and their wheels like a whirlwind:

verses.latin_vulgate: Rugitus ejus ut leonis ;  rugiet ut catuli leonum :  et frendet, et tenebit prædam,  et amplexabitur, et non erit qui eruat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁאָגָ֥ה ל֖וֹ כַּלָּבִ֑יא (ושאג) [יִשְׁאַ֨ג] כַּכְּפִירִ֤ים וְיִנְהֹם֙ וְיֹאחֵ֣ז טֶ֔רֶף וְיַפְלִ֖יט וְאֵ֥ין מַצִּֽיל׃
verses.king_james: Their roaring shall be like a lion, they shall roar like young lions: yea, they shall roar, and lay hold of the prey, and shall carry it away safe, and none shall deliver it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et sonabit super eum in die illa sicut sonitus maris :  aspiciemus in terram,  et ecce tenebræ tribulationis,  et lux obtenebrata est in caligine ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיִנְהֹ֥ם עָלָ֛יו בַּיּ֥וֹם הַה֖וּא כְּנַהֲמַת־יָ֑ם וְנִבַּ֤ט לָאָ֙רֶץ֙ וְהִנֵּה־חֹ֔שֶׁךְ צַ֣ר וָא֔וֹר חָשַׁ֖ךְ בַּעֲרִיפֶֽיהָ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And in that day they shall roar against them like the roaring of the sea: and if one look unto the land, behold darkness and sorrow, and the light is darkened in the heavens thereof.
